title: ruppia maritima var. intermedia
intro: Ruppia maritima var. intermedia, also known as intermediate sea bladderwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Ruppiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which help to soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Ruppia maritima var. intermedia include relieving coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial properties. Ruppia maritima var. intermedia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
